description: Kafka 4.x KRaft fleet and cluster operations dashboard built on Kafka JMX, kafka_exporter 1.9.0, node metrics, alerts, and VictoriaLogs. Select cls=All for the global fleet view.

panels.description: Topic shape at a glance — partitions (`kafka_topic_partitions`), replication factor and min in-sync replicas (max/min across partitions), under-replicated and non-preferred-leader partition counts, and retained record span (end − oldest offsets, records not bytes). Red/amber strips mean the topic deviates from its declared durability or placement.

targets.expr: max(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partitions{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic="$topic"})) or on() vector(0)
max(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_replicas{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic="$topic"})) or on() vector(0)
min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_in_sync_replica{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic="$topic"})) or on() vector(0)
sum(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic="$topic"})) or on() vector(0)
sum(1 - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_leader_is_preferred{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic="$topic"})) or on() vector(0)

sum(clamp_min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic="$topic"}) - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic="$topic"}), 0)) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Leader, preferred-leader state, replicas, ISR, offsets, retention span, and worst consumer lag per partition.
panels.title: Partition Topology

panels.description: Partitions of $topic whose ISR is smaller than the replica set (`kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition` summed, deduplicated). Red ≥1 — those partitions run with reduced durability margin.

targets.expr: sum(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Partitions whose current leader is not the preferred first replica (`1 − kafka_topic_partition_leader_is_preferred` summed). Amber ≥1: load is likely skewed after a failover; preferred-leader election restores intended placement.

targets.expr: sum(1 - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_leader_is_preferred{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Total missing in-sync replicas across partitions of $topic (`replicas − in_sync_replica` summed, clamped ≥0). Unlike the URP partition count, this weights each partition by HOW MANY replicas are missing.

targets.expr: sum(clamp_min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_replicas{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}) - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_in_sync_replica{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}), 0)) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Sum of log-end minus oldest offsets. This is an offset span, not an exact record count for compacted topics.

targets.expr: sum(clamp_min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}) - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}), 0)) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Retained records of $topic: `current_offset − oldest_offset` summed over partitions. A record count, not bytes — Kafka exposes no per-topic byte size through the bounded JMX contract, so span is the volume proxy.

targets.expr: sum(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_consumergroup_members{job="$job",cls="$cls",consumergroup=~"$group"})) or on() vector(0)

panels.description: Unconsumed records in $topic across ALL consumer groups (`kafka:csg_topic:lag` summed over groups — the alert-grade lag rule). Amber ≥10k, orange ≥100k, red ≥1M.

Append rate per partition (`rate(kafka_topic_partition_current_offset)` clamped ≥0, deduplicated). With keyed producers, persistent imbalance here is key skew — the hottest partition caps effective topic throughput and consumer parallelism.
panels.title: Partition Append Rate

panels.description: Per-partition, per-group lag on $topic (`kafka_consumergroup_lag`, deduplicated). Divergence BETWEEN partitions inside one group = hot partition or stuck consumer; divergence BETWEEN groups = one application falling behind while others keep up.
panels.title: Consumer Lag by Partition

panels.description: Partition offset positions from kafka_exporter: log end offset (`kafka_topic_partition_current_offset`) vs oldest retained offset (`kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset`). The vertical gap is retained records per partition; parallel slopes mean balanced append load; oldest rising = retention deleting segments.
panels.title: Partition Offsets
Partition Retained Span
panels.description: Retained records per partition: `current_offset − oldest_offset` (deduplicated, clamped ≥0). Sudden drops are retention deleting whole segments — normal and stepwise. Persistently uneven spans on a keyed topic record the history of append skew; a span pinned at zero is an empty or fully-expired partition.

panels.title: Commit Rate by Group
panels.description: Records committed per second on $topic by each consumer group (`kafka:csg_topic:commit_rate5m`). Read together with the lag panel on the right: a group with high lag AND high commit rate is draining a backlog; high lag with zero commit rate has stopped consuming.

Total backlog per consumer group: `kafka:csg_topic:lag` (deduplicated, per-topic recording rule) summed by group, topk $topk. This is the exact series the KafkaConsumerLagGrowing alert evaluates (>100k records and still growing after 30m).
panels.title: Consumer Lag by Group

panels.description: Exact committed offset and lag for every selected consumer group and partition of the selected topic. Empty when no matching consumer groups exist.
panels.title: Consumer Partition Offsets

panels.description: Topic partitioning, replication, leadership, offset span, and append progress. RF is the maximum replica count; MinISR is the minimum observed in-sync replica count.

targets.expr: label_join(max by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_replicas{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(min by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_in_sync_replica{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_under_replicated_partition{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (1 - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_leader_is_preferred{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")
label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"})), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")

label_join(sum by (cls, topic) (clamp_min(max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_current_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}) - max without (ins, ip, instance) (kafka_topic_partition_oldest_offset{job="$job",cls="$cls",topic=~"$topic"}), 0)), "key", " / ", "cls", "topic")

panels.title: Topic Inventory

panels.description: Alert timeline in the standard Pigsty style: solid bands are firing alerts (`ALERTS{alertstate="firing"}`), pale bands are pending ones still inside their for-window, for every alert carrying this cluster label. Click a legend entry to inspect it in Alertmanager.

kafka_exporter replica liveness (`up{job="kafka",role=""}` per replica): teal while the replica answers scrapes, red when down. Two replicas scrape the same cluster for redundancy and their logical series are deduplicated in every query, so losing one replica does not lose data — losing both blinds topic/partition/consumer panels while brokers keep serving.
panels.title: Exporter Availability

panels.description: kafka_exporter runtime cost: resident memory (`process_resident_memory_bytes`), Go heap in use, scrape duration (right axis, `scrape_duration_seconds`) and samples per scrape. Rising duration tracks growing cardinality; hitting the scrape timeout surfaces as KafkaExporterDown flaps.
panels.title: Exporter Memory / Scrape Cost

targets.expr: job:syslog ip:in(${members:doublequote}) (unit:kafka OR app:kafka) | fields level,ip,unit,_msg

panels.title: Kafka Service Logs
panels.description: Raw Kafka server logs from VictoriaLogs: LogsQL `job:syslog (unit:kafka OR app:kafka)` filtered to the member IPs of this scope (log streams carry node identity, so filtering is by ip, not by kafka cls). Fields: level, ip, unit, message.

title: Kafka Topic
